exam.title: Claude Certified Architect – Foundations
exam.cost: Free for first 5,000 Anthropic partner employees; $99/attempt thereafter
target_candidate: Solution architects building production applications with Claude
Senior AI engineers implementing multi-agent systems
Consulting firm staff deploying enterprise Claude integrations
validated_competencies: Design and implement agentic systems using Claude Agent SDK
Build multi-agent orchestration with hub-and-spoke patterns
Configure Claude Code for development workflows and CI/CD
Design effective tools and integrate MCP servers
Apply production-grade prompt engineering and structured output techniques
Manage context and reliability in long-running sessions
Implement programmatic hooks for deterministic business rule enforcement
Design escalation and error propagation patterns

tasks.title: Design agentic loops using the Agent SDK

skills.description: Implement correct agentic loop termination by inspecting stop_reason rather than using arbitrary iteration caps.
techniques: Checking stop_reason ('tool_use' vs 'end_turn') for loop control
Letting the agentic loop terminate naturally
Avoiding hardcoded max_iterations as primary stopping mechanism

skills.description: Structure the core messages/tools API call pattern for reliable agent execution.
techniques: Tool result injection into message history

skills.description: Design hub-and-spoke coordinator/subagent architectures for parallel task execution.
Manage context passing and isolation between agents in multi-agent pipelines.
techniques: Context isolation to prevent reasoning contamination
Information provenance tracking across agent hops

skills.description: Implement error propagation patterns in multi-agent systems.
techniques: Partial failure handling in parallel agent execution

skills.description: Use hooks to enforce deterministic business rules that cannot be left to model judgment.
techniques: Compliance enforcement via hooks vs prompt instructions

skills.description: Choose between prompt-based and programmatic enforcement based on criticality.
techniques: Using hooks for critical, deterministic rules
Using prompts for flexible, judgment-based guidelines
Anti-pattern: relying on prompts for PII redaction or legal compliance

skills.description: Design session management for long-running agentic workflows.
techniques: Session isolation for independent task branches

skills.description: Decompose complex tasks for agentic execution.

tasks.title: Design effective tool descriptions and schemas

skills.description: Write tool descriptions that enable reliable model selection in multi-tool environments.
techniques: Parameter schema design with clear types and constraints
Keeping tool count manageable (4-5 per agent) for reliable selection

skills.description: Design structured error responses that enable agent recovery.
techniques: Providing actionable context in error messages
Distinguishing access failures from empty results

skills.description: Configure MCP servers for Claude Code and Agent SDK environments.
techniques: Scoping MCP servers to project vs user level

skills.description: Select appropriate tool distribution strategy: built-in tools vs MCP vs inline tool definitions.
techniques: When to use built-in tools (Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep)
When to create custom MCP servers

skills.description: Select the correct built-in tool for common development tasks.
techniques: Read for file reading vs Bash for shell commands
WebSearch and WebFetch for external information

skills.description: Design CLAUDE.md files at appropriate levels of the directory hierarchy for team and project scoping.
techniques: Global (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) vs project vs subdirectory scoping

skills.description: Write effective CLAUDE.md content for consistent agent behavior.
Create slash commands for frequently used workflows.
Build reusable skills for cross-project agent capabilities.
techniques: Skills vs CLAUDE.md vs slash commands: choosing the right abstraction

tasks.title: Use plan mode and iterative refinement

skills.description: Choose between plan mode and direct execution based on task risk and complexity.
techniques: Plan mode for destructive or complex multi-step operations
Direct execution for well-understood, reversible tasks
Iterative refinement loops for quality-sensitive output

tasks.title: Integrate Claude Code into CI/CD pipelines

skills.description: Run Claude Code non-interactively in automated pipelines.
techniques: Using -p flag for non-interactive prompt execution

skills.description: Use Batch API for high-volume Claude Code operations.
Design prompts with explicit evaluation criteria for consistent, measurable output.
Use few-shot prompting for output consistency in extraction and classification tasks.
Use tool_use (forced tool call) to guarantee structured JSON output instead of parsing natural language.
techniques: tool_choice: {type: 'tool', name: 'extract_data'} for forced structured output
JSON schema design for extraction tasks
Schema validation against Pydantic or JSON Schema

skills.description: Implement validation-retry loops for robust structured output pipelines.
techniques: Max retry limits with fallback to human review

skills.description: Apply multi-pass review strategies using separate sessions to avoid self-review bias.
techniques: Generation and review in separate sessions
Reviewer prompt design for objective critique

skills.description: Position critical information in context for optimal model attention.
techniques: Avoiding progressive summarization that loses key details

skills.description: Manage context degradation in long sessions.
techniques: Detecting context drift in extended workflows

tasks.title: Design escalation and human review patterns

skills.description: Implement escalation based on programmatic criteria rather than model-reported confidence.
techniques: Anti-pattern: escalating based on sentiment or self-reported confidence scores

skills.description: Track information provenance in multi-agent pipelines.

scenarios.title: Customer Support Resolution Agent
scenarios.description: Design an AI-powered customer support agent handling inquiries, issue resolution, and escalation.

scenarios.title: Code Generation with Claude Code
scenarios.description: Configure Claude Code for development team workflows with custom commands and TDD patterns.

scenarios.title: Multi-Agent Research System
scenarios.description: Build a coordinator-subagent system for parallel research tasks with context isolation.

scenarios.title: Developer Productivity with Claude
scenarios.description: Create developer tools using the Claude Agent SDK with built-in tools and MCP servers.

scenarios.title: Claude Code for CI/CD
scenarios.description: Integrate Claude Code into continuous integration and delivery pipelines.

scenarios.title: Structured Data Extraction
scenarios.description: Build a structured data extraction pipeline from unstructured documents.

anti_patterns.anti_pattern: Parsing natural language for loop termination
Arbitrary iteration caps as primary stopping mechanism
anti_patterns.correct_approach: Let the agentic loop terminate naturally via stop_reason

anti_patterns.anti_pattern: Prompt-based enforcement for critical business rules
anti_patterns.correct_approach: Use programmatic hooks for deterministic enforcement
Use structured criteria and programmatic checks
Escalate based on task complexity and policy gaps
Include isError, errorCategory, isRetryable, and context
Distinguish access failures from genuinely empty results
Keep to 4-5 tools per agent for optimal selection
Use separate sessions to avoid reasoning context bias
Track accuracy per document type to catch masked failures

anti_patterns.anti_pattern: Using natural language for structured output
anti_patterns.correct_approach: Use tool_choice forced tool call to guarantee JSON schema compliance
Structured summarization preserving key facts and provenance
